programmes.show_description: Jack is convinced that something is going on between May and Cecil - and with good reason as they knew each other 18 years earlier and May happens to have an 18-year-old daughter. S2 E2: Father's Day
Gary takes Michael out in his pram and is nearly killed by a bomb. When he awakes in the present day, he is horrified to discover Michael has also travelled in time. S5 E1: A Room with a View
Mr. Bean goes to the funfair and ends up having to watch over a baby; will the mother find them in time? E10: Mind the Baby, Mr. Bean
A visitor from Gene Hunt's old stomping ground of Manchester has a surprise in store, and Alex is desperate to get to the bottom of the corruption she believes is in the force. S2 E4
Sandra isn't sure Derek is who she thought he is; she is happy that he saves animals, but she hates the idea of not saving them. S8 E5: They Decide Up There What Goes On Down Here
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
With a head full of compassion, Brenda has ideas about making Pamela feel more appreciated by her lover Sidney Clough. S1 E6: Repenting
Tracey and Sharon take a trip to a spa to cope with Tracey's shattering news and try to make plans for the future, and Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter. S11 E8: Spa Wars
Rowan Atkinson stars as the hapless Mr. Bean. In this original award-winning episode, he takes an exam, sunbathes and goes to church with Richard Briers. Ep1: Mr. Bean
David goes to a casino where he meets the Cochrans and hopes that Linda will bring him luck as he puts his winning system into play. S2 E2: Casino

Connie and Oswald join the holiday to see Billy and Aveline, and Joey's protest about the game-shooting on the estate wins him an admirer. S8 E8
Sandra likes Derek; she would marry him and start a family with him, but he doesn't know she has feelings for him. Carol is worried for her mum's health. S8 E3: You've No Idea What I've Been Through
Jack finds a bag stuffed full of money and has grand plans for a life of luxury, but they soon fall flat. S2 E3: The Gravy Train
Lord Meldrum holds a musical evening, a royal party is invited to a dinner party, and a surprise invitee is expected to attend later in the evening. S2 E7 Royal Flush
When Chrissy is harassed by an intimidating tall man at the pub, Robin decides that he will learn self-defence and take the man on the next time he sees him. S2 E2: Colour Me Yellow
Ali and Su-Lee surprise the class when they announce that they will be getting married. S1 E8: Better to Have Loved and Lost
Life in the Reads' household is very different for Alf - especially when, as he gets back from work, Min is in no rush to make his dinner. S7 E2: Min the Housekeeper

When his wife goes missing, Arthur is under suspicion, but he's got a more important cash flow matter to contend with. S8 E3: Whatever Happened to Her Indoors?

Phoebe finds living in the West End difficult, though Noel Coward does his best to help. Yvonne puts Gary on the guest list for a Downing Street party. S5 E2: London Pride
When the hapless bumbler Mr. Bean goes back to school, he scares a boy in the chemistry lab and the nudes in the art class cause him to look at the floor. E11: Back to School, Mr. Bean
When a violent burglary occurs at Alex's in-laws' house, she comes face-to-face with their son Peter, the 14-year-old future father of Molly. S2 E5
Sandra is preparing for marriage and trying to get into shape, and Lucian is very upset that someone has stolen all his rabbits. S8 E6: The Edge

Brenda and Malcolm go for a spot of bird watching in Malcolm's hide, but Malcolm ends up flat on his back when the hide collapses; meanwhile, Oswald appears again in his cafe. S1 E7: Hiding
When Darryl dies, Tracey has to decide what kind of a send-off to give him - Sharon wants it to be nice for the boys and Dorien has got the perfect outfit. S12 E1: Knocking on Heaven's Door
Mr. Bean turns the most ordinary situations into moments of excruciating embarrassment as he visits a department store and gets carried away. E2: The Return of Mr. Bean
When a young English couple arrive at the hotel, Amy and Linda assume that they are newlyweds because they are so evidently in love. S2 E3: Couples

Adrian, who has been writing his memoirs, makes a decision that will change his life. Joey has reached the end of his tether. S8 E9

Sandra is engaged to Derek and goes ring shopping, but she ends up taking one by mistake; Carol contacts Derek to help solve what could be a massive problem for Sandra. S8 E4: God Bless Us and Save Us
British Railways issues Mr Parkin with a van for his job, but he needs to learn how to drive it. S2 E4: The Van
Devious butler Alf chances upon a moneymaking scheme, and Sir Ralph suspects that Lady Agatha is seeing another man and Lord Meldrum is asked to help him. S3 E1 Please Help the Orphans
Chrissy's new boyfriend wants to take her to Bournemouth for the weekend, but Robin discovers something about him that he thinks might make Chrissy want to reconsider. S2 E3: In Praise of Older Men
Mr. Brown becomes ill and cannot come to class. The students all pay him a visit, each with their own 'remedy' to make him feel better. S1 E9: Kill or Cure
Min orders Bert to lose weight by jogging. Alf keeps pace on a bike, but before long they end up in the pub. S7 E3: Drunk in Charge of a Bicycle
